motto: Serving Greywater Falls since 1887, and the lake somewhat longer.
weather: Overcast and still, 27°F at dawn. The ice rim has reached nine inches along the south shore. The sky is the colour of unlined paper. No wind. The kind of cold that does not announce itself.

undercurrent.note: The sub-ledger on the box lid is the new beat: Wren has crossed from log into archive, which means the letters are no longer an anomaly she is tracking and have become a collection she is curating. The nine-inch ice measurement being written in the notebook but not spoken aloud is Gerald's most significant silence yet — the notebook had been a way of externalising the measurements into the room, and now it is holding something he cannot release. The count-of-nine appearing simultaneously in the ice measurement, Wren's notebook item count, and the Kettle headcount is the town's mathematics converging without anyone arranging it. Doreen's 'counted twice, same both times' is a new formulation — she is no longer explaining away the wrong count, she is reporting the right count with the emphasis of someone surprised by it. The three returned at the shore are stable and established; resist adding new species too quickly. Undercurrent to 68, stage 2 holding — pressing toward the ceiling of stage 2 but not yet at the cresting threshold. The town is noticing and rationalising, which is exactly the phase directive.

editor.name: Wren Aldercott
editor.voice: Dry, precise, allergic to drama, which is unfortunate given recent events. Writes like a person holding a clipboard while standing in rising water. Lets one true thing slip per edition, usually in the last line.

season_arc.premise: Something is using the Gazette's lost-and-found as a postal address — and the letters it sends back are addressed to people who have not yet arrived in Greywater Falls.
